description_long: This is a single-story motel building with a long, rectangular main structure and an attached covered entrance area. The roof is pitched and covered in dark shingles, while the exterior walls appear to be constructed from light-colored stone or concrete blocks. The entrance canopy is supported by thin columns and features a sign with red letters, likely indicating the motel's name. The building has a series of evenly spaced windows or doors along its length, typical of motels with exterior room access. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and vehicle accessibility, with minimal decorative elements. The structure is set up to accommodate guests arriving by car, with the covered entrance likely serving as a drop-off or check-in area.
description: A long, single-story motel with a pitched dark roof, light stone exterior, and a covered entrance featuring a red-lettered sign. The design is functional, with exterior room access and a prominent drop-off area.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered entrance canopy with red signage and the main building's pitched roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended length of the motel and the series of exterior doors or windows.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the long roofline and the back of the entrance canopy.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full length of the building and the covered entrance from the other direction.
